analysis_1_within_C_matched_comparison.description: The PRIMARY, non-confounded causal-reach evidence. Uses C's own natural alternation between delivered and undelivered PC events as a built-in treatment/no-treatment split inside ONE consistent engine -- avoids any need to compare against B for this specific question.
analysis_1_within_C_matched_comparison.interpretation: When PC's spike successfully triggers an L1I delivery, sensory firing (L1E_i) and ALL L2 spiking (not just the natural leader -- literally every L2E, including tied rivals) are completely and deterministically blocked at the very next step, in every one of 12,600 traced events across 7 seeds. When it does not deliver, nothing is altered. There is no partial, probabilistic, or threshold-marginal case anywhere in this trace -- the underlying dynamics are fully deterministic given fixed config and topology, so the causal reach is exactly 100% or exactly 0%, never in between.

tied_seed_2.note: any_l2e_fire_rate_given_delivered=0.0 means BOTH tied competitors (L2E0 and L2E3, which co-fire on every undelivered/normal step) are blocked TOGETHER on delivered/suppressed steps -- suppression removes the shared feedforward drive both compete for, rather than differentially favoring one over the other. This is the direct mechanistic reason a real tie is not broken by suppression.

analysis_2_B_vs_C_whole_regime_compariso.correction_to_prior_report: The mature-efficacy report's 'B_l1i_events=0' metric measured only the SELECTIVE delivery count (necessarily zero when prediction_column_to_i_enabled=False), not total L1I activity. B's L1I is, in fact, actively firing throughout via the pre-existing legacy pathway. This does not change that experiment's verdict (which was about sensory/PC-layer activity and ownership, both still correctly measured), but the specific phrase 'B delivers 0 L1I events' should be read as 'B delivers 0 PC-driven L1I events', not 'B's L1I never fires'.
analysis_2_B_vs_C_whole_regime_compariso.description: SECONDARY, explicitly caveated. B's L1I is not silent -- it receives the legacy global L2E-winner broadcast every step (inh.receive_input(l2e, t=t)), a completely different and, empirically, MORE frequent trigger than C's PC-driven path (B fired L1I 2250 times across 3 pixels over 1500 steps vs C's 900). This means B and C's L1E/L2E trajectories can diverge for reasons unrelated to any specific PC event -- this comparison answers 'does the whole selective-vs-global regime differ', not 'does this PC event reach L2', and must not be read as isolating PC's causal contribution the way analysis 1 does.
analysis_2_B_vs_C_whole_regime_compariso.ownership_across_all_7_seeds: unchanged in every seed (B and C's natural leader identity, or tied pair, is always identical)

builds_on: /home/cxiong/codex-runs/claude-phase35-mature-efficacy/ (same checkpoint, same Stage 1 maturation code, same clone_engine/make_bc B-vs-C construction, reused unchanged)
method: two explicitly separate analyses -- see report.md for why they must not be conflated
processes_remaining: none from this audit; every python3 process it started (7 causal_trace.py runs) completed and exited before this file was written. Confirmed via ps -u cxiong.

requested_aggregate_fractions.note_on_the_0.5s: 0.5 is not a partial/probabilistic effect size -- it is exactly the delivery rate (does L1I cross threshold this event or not), and conditional on delivery the effect is deterministically 100%. The membrane-but-not-firing fraction is exactly 0.0 because a delivered suppression event drives ff_vec to zero for all 3 active pixels together (they suppress in lockstep, sharing timing), so L2E receives literally no new feedforward charge that step at all -- there is no intermediate case where charge moves but stays sub-threshold; charge simply does not move.

seeds_traced.note: seed 2 was deliberately added for this audit specifically because it produces a persistent exact tie between L2E0 and L2E3 (see the mature-efficacy calibration log) -- the 6 clean seeds alone cannot distinguish 'suppression can't redirect ownership because there's no real competition to redirect' from 'suppression can't redirect ownership even when real competition exists'; seed 2 tests the second, harder question directly.

chain: t: PC_i resolves coincidence using data queued prediction_feedback_delay steps ago; if it fires, pcol_spiked[i]=1 this step
t: if prediction_column_to_i_enabled, L1I_i receives pcol_spiked[i] this same step (line ~2826)
t: L1I_i may or may not cross its own threshold and fire this step, depending on accumulated charge (see below)
t (end of step): self.l1i_feedback_delay = l1i.copy() -- L1I's fire outcome THIS step is stored in a register (line 2909)
t+1 (start of next step): L1E_i's receive_input reads l1i_feedback_delay[i] as this step's inhibitory event (line 2584) -- ONE STEP LATER than the L1I fire that caused it

t+1 (same step): L1E_i's resulting firing state directly builds ff_vec, which is delivered to every L2E as feedforward drive IN THE SAME STEP (lines 2694-2696, 2744-2748) -- there is no additional delay between L1E's decision and L2's use of it
step_ordering_established_by_code_readin.l1i_accumulation: L1I's weight (after the necessary L1I-topology reshape documented in the mature-efficacy report) is below its own threshold (thr_l1i=8000; reshaped weight ~4600-5000 depending on seed) -- a single PC-driven delivery is insufficient to fire L1I by itself. With L1I_FEEDBACK_REFRACTORY=2 and l1i_leak_enabled=False (default, untouched), charge accumulates undecayed across non-consecutive deliveries until a second delivery crosses threshold, then L1I fires and resets. Empirically this produces a clean, deterministic alternation: every PC spike event either delivers (L1I crosses threshold, ~50% of events) or does not (~50% of events), confirmed identically across all 7 seeds traced.
step_ordering_established_by_code_readin.source: backend/simulation.py step(), lines ~2538-2923 (read in full before any tracing code was written)

verdict_rationale: Every one of 12,600 traced PC-spike-derived events across 7 seeds (6 clean-leader, 1 genuinely tied) shows the same deterministic pattern: when PC's spike successfully triggers an L1I delivery (50% of events, gated by L1I's own 2-event accumulation requirement), sensory firing and ALL L2 spiking are completely blocked the very next step -- not delayed, not merely reduced, not marginal to threshold. The mechanism reaches L2 with full causal fidelity every time it fires. It never changes ownership in any seed, including the tied one, because it suppresses the shared upstream sensory drive that every competing L2E depends on equally rather than favoring any one of them -- it can only change how much activity happens, never who wins when activity does happen.

why_20_percent_sensory_reduction_produce.chosen_cause: mixed of two specific, evidenced mechanisms, but the dominant one supports a single clean verdict
why_20_percent_sensory_reduction_produce.mechanism_1_dominant: Suppression acts on the SHARED sensory input layer (L1E_i), which is upstream of and common to every L2E competing for that pixel. When it fires, it removes the entire feedforward drive for that step for ALL competitors simultaneously and equally (confirmed directly in the tied seed: both L2E0 and L2E3 are blocked together, not selectively). A mechanism that cannot differentially favor one competitor over another cannot, by construction, redirect which one wins -- it can only modulate how often the race happens at all.
why_20_percent_sensory_reduction_produce.mechanism_2_contributing: In the 6 clean-leader seeds specifically, there is also no real competitor present to redirect ownership toward even if suppression were selective -- the natural leader wins essentially 100% of its own undelivered opportunities against negligible rivalry, so skipping alternating steps changes tempo, not outcome. Seed 2 (tied) was traced specifically to separate this from mechanism 1, and shows mechanism 1 is sufficient on its own: even with two genuine, exactly-tied competitors present, suppression still cannot break the tie, because it removes both competitors' drive together rather than one relative to the other.

rejected_explanations.reaches_L2_but_insufficient_for_threshol: false -- there is no threshold-marginal case; delivered events remove ALL feedforward charge, not a partial amount
rejected_explanations.reaches_L2_too_late: false -- reach is same-step (the suppressed L1E state feeds L2's feedforward drive in the identical step), not delayed relative to the L2 decision
rejected_explanations.structurally_decoupled_from_first_race: false -- suppression is directly, deterministically, causally coupled to the race's raw material (feedforward drive) every single time it is delivered; 'decoupled' would overstate the disconnect
